title: Tribunals of Mythic Europe
note: The thirteen regional Tribunals (Core Rules Ch.2). Roll for where a covenant, rival, or Order matter lies, or to colour a region. Most have a dedicated sourcebook in the repo corpus (reviewed/ and wip/).

entries.value: Rhine — Germany and the great forests of central Europe; tradition-bound, gauntlet-strict.
Normandy — northern France; crowded, competitive, tournament-style Tribunal politics.
Provençal (Provence) — southern France and the Pyrenees; courtly love, Cathar shadows.
Stonehenge — England and Wales; conquest-haunted, ancient sites.
Loch Leglean — Scotland; wild highlands and hedge traditions.
Hibernia — Ireland; deep faerie presence and old covenants.
Greater Alps — the Alpine arc; isolated, vis-rich, treacherous passes.
Iberia — Spain and Portugal; the Christian–Muslim frontier and the Reconquista.
Rome — the Italian peninsula; the Pope's shadow and the ruins of antiquity.
Theban — Greece and the Aegean; Byzantine learning, Mystery cults, initiation.
Transylvania — eastern Europe and the Carpathians; House Tremere's stronghold.
Novgorod — the Rus' lands and the northeast; frozen frontier and pagan survivals.
Levant — the Crusader states and the Holy Land; Outremer, Crusade, and strong Dominion.
